title: hibbertia stricta var. calycina
intro: Hibbertia stricta var. calycina, also known as the yellow guinea flower, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Western Australia. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Dilleniaceae, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and small, oval-shaped leaves. Hibbertia stricta var. calycina has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which have been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
